mcpTools.name: mcp_context7_resolve-library-id
mcpTools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns a list of matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'get-library-docs' to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Trust score (consider libraries with scores of 7-10 more authoritative)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID.

mcpTools.name: mcp_context7_get-library-docs
mcpTools.description: Fetches up-to-date documentation for a library. You must call 'resolve-library-id' first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

context7CompatibleLibraryID.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens of documentation to retrieve (default: 10000). Higher values provide more context but consume more tokens.

mcpTools.name: mcp_code_explainer
mcpTools.description: Explains code snippets or files with detailed analysis of functionality, patterns, and potential issues.

code.description: The code snippet or file content to explain.

language.description: The programming language of the code (e.g., 'javascript', 'python', 'typescript').

detail_level.description: The level of detail for the explanation.

mcpTools.name: mcp_dependency_analyzer
mcpTools.description: Analyzes project dependencies for vulnerabilities, outdated packages, and optimization opportunities.

package_file.description: Path to the package.json, requirements.txt, or other dependency file.

mcpTools.name: mcp_test_generator
mcpTools.description: Generates comprehensive test cases for functions, components, or modules.

code.description: The code to generate tests for.

mcpTools.name: mcp_code_reviewer
mcpTools.description: Performs automated code reviews to identify bugs, anti-patterns, and improvement opportunities.

language.description: The programming language of the code.

focus_areas.description: Areas to focus the review on.

mcpTools.name: mcp_api_documentation
mcpTools.description: Generates comprehensive API documentation from code or existing API specs.

source.description: The source code or API specification to document.

format.description: The output format for the documentation.
